sentence: She usually goes shopping on Saturday mornings
explanation: Frequency adverbs like <usually> go before the main verb.

sentence: They often play tennis after work
explanation: Frequency adverbs go before the main verb: <often play>.

sentence: He always takes the bus to the office
explanation: <Always> goes before the main verb: <always takes>.

sentence: We sometimes eat out at a restaurant on Fridays
hint: Subject + adverb + phrasal verb + place + time
explanation: <Sometimes> goes before the main verb. <Eat out> means to eat at a restaurant.

sentence: I never drink coffee in the evening
explanation: <Never> goes before the main verb: <never drink>.

sentence: She is currently learning to play the guitar
hint: Subject + is + adverb + verb-ing + infinitive + object
explanation: Present continuous with adverb: <is currently learning>.

sentence: They usually have breakfast at eight o'clock
explanation: Frequency adverbs go before the main verb: <usually have>.

sentence: He rarely watches television during the week
explanation: <Rarely> goes before the main verb: <rarely watches>.

sentence: We always walk to school together in the morning
hint: Subject + adverb + verb + destination + manner + time
explanation: <Always> goes before the main verb: <always walk>.

sentence: She often reads a book before going to bed
hint: Subject + adverb + verb + object + time clause
explanation: <Often> goes before the main verb: <often reads>.

sentence: I sometimes go for a walk in the park after lunch
hint: Subject + adverb + verb + activity + place + time
explanation: <Go for a walk> is a common expression for walking as exercise.

sentence: He usually does his homework before dinner
explanation: <Usually> goes before the main verb: <usually does>.
